prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, portrait orientation
Primary request: Warhammer 40,000 / Dark Imperium inspired grimdark planetary war-zone atlas for the terrain type diverse_archipelago.
Scene/backdrop: a vast toxic ocean war world filled with many island groups and microcontinents, hive city islands, underhive slum coasts, forge-world archipelagos, promethium refinery platforms, industrial wastelands, orbital landing islands, ruined battle sectors, irradiated reef belts, quarantined xenos zones and mechanicus sensor stations.
Style/medium: high-resolution grimdark far-future atlas, imperial sector cartography translated into planetary ocean map, gothic architecture aesthetic, toxic ochre water, mechanicus technical overlay, armored parchment-metal border, brutal war-zone mood.
Composition/framing: tall portrait world map with unevenly distributed island clusters; include large hive islands, tiny refinery islets, crescent island arcs, toxic lagoons, industrial reef belts, narrow straits, remote atolls, fragmented cliffs and microcontinents with no grid-like spacing; include auspex compass, orbital route lines, hazard glyphs, hive icons and tactical sector boundaries without readable text.
Geography constraints: islands must vary strongly in size and shape, with realistic complex coastlines, broken shores, curved bays, peninsulas, polluted deltas, toxic estuaries, ash fjords, volcanic forge ridges and wreck-strewn coasts; show many climates and regions including polar wastes, rad deserts, toxic swamps, industrial basins, hive sprawl, mountain fortresses and blasted battlefields.
Avoid: evenly spaced islands, clean bright sci-fi, cheerful tropical palette, modern satellite image, readable labels, UI, watermark, logo, real-world map copy.
